title: Add hostname to /etc/hosts
body:
This can fix #1745 
Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

We need tests on this.
Make sure it works correctly with different network flags.

--net host
--net none
--net container:othercontainer

Also make sure it works when you create/start a container.

Needs a test to 
run a container named `test`
Check hostname field.
Run a different container in the back ground.  This should grab the same IP as the previous container.
Start the `test` container again, and make sure it has a different IP then the first time it was run.

> Run a different container in the back ground. This should grab the same IP as the previous container.

I think in podman container can grab different IP, different from docker?
I think I can add test to check the IPAddress are different.
And chaeck the ip of `test` has been changed after the 2nd start in `/etc//hosts`

I don't think we want to rely on the specific address allocation behavior
of CNI, so I agree with Qi here - let's just test that the IP is added to
hosts and it matches what we get out of inspect

On Sat, Nov 3, 2018, 09:56 Qi Wang <notifications@github.com wrote:

> Run a different container in the back ground. This should grab the same IP
> as the previous container.
>
> I think in podman container can grap different IP, different from docker?
> I think I can add test to check the IPAddress are different.
> And chaeck the ip of test has been changed after the 2nd start
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you commented.

> .

@QiWang19 Fine. I just want to see if we can tests that a second start of a container that happens to get a different IP will work correctly and add the new IP to the /etc/hosts file.

I can get hostname in /etc/hosts in my laptop, but when running tests I add `session = podmanTest.Podman([]string{"run", "--hostname=foobar", ALPINE, "cat", "/etc/hosts"})` to test/e2e/run_dns_test.go, the session doesn't return that.
So my code doesn't work?
@QiWang19 Please rebase to pick up CI fixes

CI is green, and we have tests. LGTM.

@baude @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 PTAL
